architectureSummary.overview: CS PM Service is a .NET 6 microservices system with 8 background worker services communicating asynchronously via Azure Service Bus. Each service owns its own SQL Server database. A YARP API gateway routes external requests. Deployed via Docker on Azure AKS.
architectureSummary.diagramText: Client → YARP Gateway → [OrderService | InventoryService | PaymentService | NotificationService | ReportService | AuditService | SchedulerService | ConfigService] ↔ Azure Service Bus

questions.questionText: How did services communicate in the CS PM Service microservices architecture?
questions.answer:
Services communicated asynchronously via Azure Service Bus. No direct HTTP calls between services (except through the API gateway for synchronous queries). This decoupled services so a failure in one didn't cascade to others.

Messaging pattern:

// Publisher (OrderService)
public class OrderCreatedPublisher
{
    private readonly ServiceBusSender _sender;

    public async Task PublishAsync(Order order, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var evt = new OrderCreatedEvent
        {
            OrderId = order.Id,
            CustomerId = order.CustomerId,
            Items = order.Items.Select(i => new OrderItemDto(i.ProductId, i.Quantity)).ToList(),
            TotalAmount = order.TotalAmount,
            CreatedAt = order.CreatedAt
        };

        var message = new ServiceBusMessage(
            BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(evt))
        {
            MessageId = order.Id.ToString(),        // deduplication key
            CorrelationId = _correlationId,          // distributed tracing
            Subject = "OrderCreated"
        };

        await _sender.SendMessageAsync(message, ct);
    }
}

// Subscriber (InventoryService)
public class OrderCreatedConsumer : BackgroundService
{
    private readonly ServiceBusProcessor _processor;

    protected override async Task ExecuteAsync(CancellationToken stoppingToken)
    {
        _processor.ProcessMessageAsync += HandleMessageAsync;
        _processor.ProcessErrorAsync += HandleErrorAsync;
        await _processor.StartProcessingAsync(stoppingToken);
    }

    private async Task HandleMessageAsync(ProcessMessageEventArgs args)
    {
        var evt = args.Message.Body.ToObjectFromJson<OrderCreatedEvent>();
        await _inventoryService.ReserveStockAsync(evt.Items);
        await args.CompleteMessageAsync(args.Message);  // ack = delete from queue
    }
}
followUpQuestions: What happens if a consumer fails to process a message?
questions.importantForInterview: Dead-letter queue (DLQ) receives messages that fail processing after max delivery count (default 10). Monitor the DLQ — messages there indicate processing bugs. Azure Service Bus has built-in duplicate detection via MessageId — set it to the entity's natural ID.

questions.questionText: How did you configure the YARP API gateway in CS PM Service?
questions.answer:
YARP (Yet Another Reverse Proxy) is Microsoft's .NET-native reverse proxy. We configured it to route external traffic to internal services based on path prefix.

// yarp-config.json (loaded into YARP at startup)
{
  "ReverseProxy": {
    "Routes": {
      "orders-route": {
        "ClusterId": "orders-cluster",
        "Match": { "Path": "/api/orders/{**catch-all}" },
        "Transforms": [
          { "PathRemovePrefix": "/api" }  // strip /api prefix before forwarding
        ]
      },
      "inventory-route": {
        "ClusterId": "inventory-cluster",
        "Match": { "Path": "/api/inventory/{**catch-all}" }
      }
    },
    "Clusters": {
      "orders-cluster": {
        "Destinations": {
          "destination1": { "Address": "http://order-service:8080" }
        }
      },
      "inventory-cluster": {
        "Destinations": {
          "destination1": { "Address": "http://inventory-service:8080" }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
// Program.cs (Gateway service)
builder.Services.AddReverseProxy()
    .LoadFromConfig(builder.Configuration.GetSection("ReverseProxy"));

// Auth at gateway — services trust the gateway's forwarded claims
app.UseAuthentication();
app.UseAuthorization();
app.MapReverseProxy();

JWT validation happens once at the gateway. Internal services use X-Forwarded-User / X-User-Id headers populated by the gateway.
followUpQuestions: How do you handle service discovery in Docker/Kubernetes?
What is the difference between YARP and Ocelot?
questions.importantForInterview: In Docker Compose, service names are the DNS hostnames (order-service resolves to the container). In Kubernetes, use ClusterIP service names. YARP supports Kubernetes service discovery via the Yarp.ReverseProxy.ServiceDiscovery.Kubernetes package.
